reasoning: The files flagged as unsafe (autogen.sh, configure.ac, strip_fPIC.sh) are SVN metadata files incorrectly identified as threats. These files are located in a version control directory (..svnbridge), are never executed during makepkg, and contain only XML property data. The PKGBUILD, install hooks, and executable scripts show no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: qwen model misinterpreted SVN metadata files (.sh/.ac extensions) as potential supply-chain threats despite their non-executable nature and isolation from build processes.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing src/PCSX-Reloaded*/pcsxr/plugins source code (where patches are applied)
Inadequate review of GPU/texture handling in plugin source files (e.g., texture.c)
false_positives: qwen: autogen.sh/configure.ac/strip_fPIC.sh – SVN metadata files misclassified as malicious despite zero execution risk
qwen: 'Mismatched content' verdicts – overreaction to version-control artifacts in source tree
false_negatives: Both models: Insufficient scrutiny of fork() usage in peopsxgl_gpu_c.patch
Both models: Missed analysis of LD_LIBRARY_PATH in epsxe.sh (potential runtime hijack surface)
learnings: File selection: Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD (prepare/build/package) over VCS artifacts. Deprioritize hidden/dot-directories.
Assessment quality: Train models to distinguish build-executed code from version-control metadata. Avoid flagging filename/content mismatches without execution path.
Common failures: Over-alertness to: 1) VCS artifacts 2) Non-build-relevant files 3) Benign fork() usage in emulator plugins
